subject: Product Management
title: What Is an A/B Test?
description: A fast breakdown of What Is an A/B Test? for builders.

hook.text: Show half your users a green button and half a red button. Measure which gets more clicks.

buildup.text: An A/B test splits users into two groups randomly. Group A sees the current version (control).

discovery.text: By comparing the metric (clicks, signups, revenue) between groups, you isolate the effect of the change from everything else.

twist.text: The key word is 'randomly.' Without random assignment, the groups may differ in ways that confuse the results — selection bias.

climax.text: A/B testing is the gold standard of product experimentation because it establishes causation, not just correlation.

punchline.text: Split randomly. Change one thing. Measure the difference. That's science.

quiz.question: Why is random assignment critical in A/B testing?
options: Without it, groups may differ in ways that confuse results — random assignment isolates the effect of the change
Random assignment makes the test run faster
It's a preference, not a requirement
